sanskrit: धूमेनाव्रियते वह्निर्यथादर्शो मलेन च। यथोल्बेनावृतो गर्भस्तथा तेनेदमावृतम्॥
transliteration: dhūmenāvriyate vahnir yathādarśo malena ca | yatholbenāvṛto garbhas tathā tenedam āvṛtam ||
literal_meaning: Just as fire is veiled by smoke, a mirror by dust, and an embryo by the womb, so this (knowledge) is veiled by that (lust).
contextual_meaning: Lust (kāma) is not the true self; it is a progressive obscuration—first thinning perception (smoke), then distorting reflection (dust), finally sealing off awareness (womb).
psychological_state: Pre-contemplative stage of change—intellect acknowledges the problem but the ego still defends the habit.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media for the tenth midnight hour, feeling numb yet unable to stop, sensing a gap between who you are and who you want to be.
anchor_text: Desire clouds inner vision; clear it to see your true self.
